text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
Restaging Examination — First Reassessment on Second-Line TKI Therapy

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Mr. Raymond Kowalski is a 63-year-old male with known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (cT3bN1M1) with sarcomatoid differentiation, status post cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection. He completed four cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab induction with a discordant/mixed response (pulmonary regression, nodal progression), transitioned to nivolumab maintenance, and ultimately demonstrated progressive disease in the retroperitoneal and pelvic nodal compartments at approximately nine months of total immunotherapy. He was subsequently started on cabozantinib 60 mg daily (later dose-reduced to 40 mg daily for tolerability) approximately three months prior to this examination. This CT is requested for reassessment of disease burden following initiation of second-line cabozantinib therapy.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of nonionic iodinated intravenous contrast (iopamidol 370 mg I/mL) using a portal venous phase acquisition protocol. Additional non-contrast and delayed phase acquisitions of the abdomen and pelvis were obtained for renal parenchymal and excretory phase assessment, respectively. Images were reconstructed in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes with 3 mm slice thickness. Oral contrast was not administered per standard renal protocol. Field of view encompasses the lung apices through the pubic symphysis.

COMPARISON:
Prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed approximately three months ago (at the time of confirmed progressive disease on nivolumab maintenance, used as baseline for current cabozantinib therapy). Additional comparison to the interim restaging CT performed approximately six months prior (six months into immunotherapy).

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally. No endobronchial lesion identified. No new pulmonary nodules are detected on this examination.

A nodule in the right lower lobe posterior basal segment measures 0.7 cm in long axis (previously 0.8 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation; 0.9 cm at the first post-immunotherapy restaging scan; and 1.4 cm at initial staging). This lesion has continued its gradual trajectory of size reduction and is currently at its nadir since treatment began. There is no surrounding ground-glass component, no pleural tethering, and no cavitation. Morphology is smooth and round.

A nodule in the left lower lobe superior segment measures 0.7 cm (previously 0.7 cm at cabozantinib initiation; 0.8 cm at first immunotherapy restaging). Stable in size and appearance. No internal change.

A nodule in the right upper lobe posterior segment measures 0.3 cm (unchanged from prior studies). This lesion is near the limit of reliable measurement and remains morphologically benign-appearing with stable contour.

No new pulmonary nodules are identified above the 6 mm threshold. Several subcentimeter nodules measuring less than 5 mm are present bilaterally and are unchanged in distribution and character, consistent with benign etiology (likely intrapulmonary lymph nodes or granulomata in the setting of prior 30 pack-year smoking history).

No pulmonary consolidation, lobar atelectasis, ground-glass opacification, or evidence of pneumonitis. No pleural effusion bilaterally. Pulmonary vascularity is normal; no pulmonary emboli on this contrast-enhanced examination.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinal contours are normal. No pathologic mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy identified. The anterior mediastinum is clear without thymic mass. Aorta and central pulmonary arteries are normal in caliber.

Heart and Pericardium:
Cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. Coronary artery calcification is mild to moderate in the left anterior descending and right coronary artery distributions, consistent with the patient's age and cardiovascular risk profile.

Chest Wall and Osseous Structures:
No suspicious chest wall lesion. No lytic or sclerotic osseous abnormality in the thoracic vertebral bodies, ribs, sternum, or visible shoulder girdle structures. Mild degenerative changes are present throughout the thoracic spine at multiple levels.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with a smooth margin and homogeneous enhancement pattern. No focal hepatic lesion identified on multiphasic imaging. The portal and hepatic veins are patent. No biliary ductal dilatation.

Gallbladder and Biliary System:
The gallbladder is present and normal in appearance, without gallstones, wall thickening, or pericholecystic fluid. The common bile duct measures 4 mm (within normal limits).

Spleen:
Normal in size and enhancement. No focal splenic lesion.

Pancreas:
The pancreas enhances homogeneously with no ductal dilatation, mass lesion, or peripancreatic inflammatory change.

Adrenal Glands:
The left adrenal gland is normal in morphology and attenuation. The right adrenal gland is surgically absent, consistent with prior right adrenalectomy performed at the time of cytoreductive nephrectomy.

Kidneys:
The right kidney is surgically absent. The nephrectomy bed is well-defined without a discrete soft tissue mass or hematoma. Mild fibrotic/postoperative soft tissue thickening at the right retroperitoneum is unchanged compared to prior examinations, consistent with expected postoperative change.

The left kidney is normal in size and contour. Cortical enhancement is symmetric and preserved. No hydronephrosis. No intrarenal lesion. A 4 mm simple cortical cyst is present at the left upper pole, unchanged.

Retroperitoneum — Upper Abdomen and Para-Aortic Region:
The previously identified aortocaval nodal conglomerate, which measured 3.4 cm at the time of peak progression on nivolumab and 3.4 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation (used as baseline for current treatment), now measures approximately 2.6 cm in its greatest transverse dimension. This represents a meaningful reduction in bulk compared to the most recent prior scan, with the nodal tissue appearing less confluent and demonstrating reduced soft tissue density compared to prior. No adjacent organ encasement or vascular involvement is appreciated.

Bowel:
No bowel obstruction, wall thickening, or mucosal abnormality. No free intraperitoneal air or fluid.

Vascular Structures:
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. The celiac, superior mesenteric, and inferior mesenteric arteries are patent. The inferior vena cava is patent; no intraluminal filling defect identified to suggest recurrent venous thrombus at the prior right renal vein surgical margin.

---

PELVIS:

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
The previously documented right common iliac nodal mass, which measured 3.1 cm at peak progression and 3.1 cm at the initiation of cabozantinib, now measures approximately 2.3 cm in greatest transverse dimension. The mass retains a somewhat ovoid configuration with heterogeneous internal enhancement but has clearly diminished in size compared to prior. No new satellite nodal tissue in the immediate vicinity.

The right obturator nodal deposit, previously measuring 1.8 cm at the time of cabozantinib initiation, now measures approximately 1.4 cm. The lesion is discrete and appears to have decreased in internal enhancement heterogeneity, possibly reflecting a favorable treatment effect.

The right external iliac node, previously measuring 1.2 cm, now measures approximately 0.9 cm. This node was noted to have appeared on the restaging scan approximately six months prior during immunotherapy and has since decreased in size.

No new pelvic lymphadenopathy above the 1.0 cm short-axis threshold is identified. The left iliac chain and inguinal lymph nodes are unremarkable.

Pelvic Organs and Soft Tissues:
The urinary bladder is well-distended and normal in wall thickness and contour. No intraluminal lesion. No hydroureter or hydronephrosis on the left. The prostate is mildly enlarged, consistent with benign prostatic hypertrophy in a 63-year-old male, without suspicious enhancement or nodule. Seminal vesicles are normal in appearance. No pelvic free fluid. No soft tissue mass in the presacral or perirectal spaces.

Osseous Structures — Pelvis and Lumbar Spine:
Multilevel lumbar degenerative disc disease with posterior osteophyte formation, most pronounced at L3-4 and L4-5. Facet arthropathy noted throughout the lumbar spine. The sacrum, iliac wings, acetabuli, and proximal femora do not demonstrate any convincing lytic or aggressive sclerotic lesion. No permeative bone destruction or cortical breakthrough. No pathologic fracture.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. RETROPERITONEAL AND PELVIC NODAL DISEASE — FAVORABLE RESPONSE TO CABOZANTINIB: The previously progressive right-sided retroperitoneal and pelvic nodal deposits have all decreased in size compared to the baseline established at the initiation of cabozantinib therapy. The aortocaval nodal conglomerate has decreased from 3.4 cm to approximately 2.6 cm. The right common iliac nodal mass has decreased from 3.1 cm to 2.3 cm. The right obturator node has decreased from 1.8 cm to 1.4 cm. The right external iliac node has decreased from 1.2 cm to 0.9 cm. Taken together, the aggregate nodal burden has decreased substantially, in keeping with a meaningful partial response in this compartment. No new regional or distant nodal disease is identified.

2. PULMONARY METASTASES — STABLE TO MARGINALLY IMPROVED: The three previously identified bilateral pulmonary nodules are stable to minimally decreased compared to the cabozantinib baseline. The right lower lobe nodule measures 0.7 cm (previously 0.8 cm); the left lower lobe nodule measures 0.7 cm (unchanged); the right upper lobe nodule measures 0.3 cm (unchanged). These lesions have been on a stable to slowly improving trajectory throughout the course of treatment. No new pulmonary nodules.

3. NO EVIDENCE OF NEW METASTATIC DISEASE: No new hepatic, adrenal, osseous, or intrathoracic lesions are identified on this examination. No suspicious bone lesions in the visualized skeleton.

4. POSTOPERATIVE CHANGES: Expected postoperative changes in the right retroperitoneum and right nephrectomy bed are stable and without evidence of local recurrence at the primary surgical site.

5. INCIDENTAL FINDINGS: Mild to moderate coronary artery calcification noted in the chest. Mild lumbar and thoracic degenerative disc and facet disease. Left upper pole simple renal cyst, unchanged. Mildly enlarged prostate, consistent with benign hypertrophy.
